title: God memory cross-device — point private namespace at a separate Supabase project
component: MemoryTools (private namespace wiring), pi_agent.py (god namespace init), .env (new GOD_SUPABASE_URL/KEY)
current_state: After T-082 closes, MemoryTools accepts `db_path` and `namespace` kwargs. For namespace != 'pi' (god), the constructor installs a _NoopSupabase shim, so god memory lives in local SQLite (data/god_memory.db) only. Ash's daily Pi access is multi-surface — desktop, Telegram bot, future web UI. Public memory (L1/L2 on Supabase) syncs across surfaces. God memory does not: switch to god mode from phone via Telegram → the local SQLite on the desktop is invisible → blank slate.
structural_problem: Three constraints in tension: (1) god is private, (2) god memory persists across sessions, (3) Pi is multi-surface. Current code satisfies 1+2 by making god desktop-local. The right answer for 1+2+3 is a *separate* Supabase project (or self-hosted Postgres) under Ash's private account that god namespace points at. Same schema, same MemoryTools code, different credentials.
target_state: Set GOD_SUPABASE_URL + GOD_SUPABASE_KEY in .env. When pi_agent constructs the private MemoryTools for god namespace, pass these instead of empty strings. The existing T-082 code path then uses a *real* Supabase client (no noop shim), and god memory becomes cross-device. Privacy boundary moves from 'never leaves the box' to 'separate account, gitignored credentials' — same privacy posture used everywhere else in this project.
files_affected: pi_agent.py — when building MemoryTools for namespace='god', read GOD_SUPABASE_URL/GOD_SUPABASE_KEY from env; fall back to empty (local-only) if absent
.env.example — document the optional keys (do NOT add real creds)
tests/test_god_cross_device.py (new) — assert private MemoryTools uses real client when creds set, noop when absent
success_criteria: GOD_SUPABASE_URL + GOD_SUPABASE_KEY in .env opt into cross-device god; absent keys = current behavior (local-only)
Switch to god mode on machine A, write a memory; switch to god mode on machine B (different physical device, same .env), read it back successfully
Real Supabase client is used when creds present; _NoopSupabase shim still used when absent
Public Supabase project never sees god rows (verify via row-count diff on raw_wiki/organized_memory)
.env.example explicitly documents these as separate-account credentials
risk_notes: Privacy regression risk if Ash accidentally points GOD_SUPABASE_URL at the same project as the public one. Mitigation: a fail-loud check in MemoryTools.__init__ that errors when namespace != 'pi' but the URL equals the public Supabase URL. Or simpler: docs make it explicit. Cost: zero on Supabase free tier; second project sits under the same Ash-account dashboard.
migration_plan: 1. Create a new private Supabase project (Ash, manual). Note URL + service_role key.
2. Apply the same SQL schema (raw_wiki, organized_memory, l3_active_memory tables) to the new project.
4. app/config.py declares them; pi_agent.py wires them into the private MemoryTools instance for namespace='god'.
5. Add fail-loud check: if GOD_SUPABASE_URL == SUPABASE_URL, refuse to start in god mode.
6. Write tests/test_god_cross_device.py mocking both URL pairs to assert isolation.
7. Manual cross-device test: write god memory on desktop, read on phone via Telegram bot, verify same content.
8. Append SOLUTIONS.jsonl with not_done (encrypted-in-public alternative, deferred indefinitely).
